claim: Mice without IFN-γ or its receptor are resistant to EAM induced with α-MyHC/CFA.

result.claim: Mice without IFN-γ or its receptor are resistant to EAM induced with α-MyHC/CFA.
result.reasoning: Evidence from paper ID 24338780, "Lethal autoimmune myocarditis in interferon-gamma receptor-deficient mice: enhanced disease severity by impaired inducible nitric oxide synthase induction," directly refutes the claim. This paper states that "IFN-gamma receptor-deficient mice (IFN-gammaR(-/-)) on a BALB/c background immunized with a peptide derived from cardiac alpha-myosin heavy chain develop severe myocarditis with high mortality." This indicates that mice lacking the IFN-γ receptor are highly susceptible, not resistant, to EAM induced with α-MyHC. Furthermore, paper ID 40632104, "Dual role of the IL-12/IFN-gamma axis in the development of autoimmune myocarditis: induction by IL-12 and protection by IFN-gamma," supports this by noting that "IFN-gamma-deficient mice developed fatal autoimmune disease."

evidence.text: Genetic ablation of cyclooxygenases (COX) in Braf(V600E) mouse melanoma cells renders them susceptible to immune control.
evidence.source: Cyclooxygenase-Dependent Tumor Growth through Evasion of Immunity

evidence.text: Mice lacking junctional adhesion molecule A (JAM-A, encoded by F11r) exhibit elevated colonic lymphocyte numbers.
evidence.source: Compromised intestinal epithelial barrier induces adaptive immune compensation that protects from colitis.

evidence.text: IL-12p40-deficient mice on a susceptible background resisted myocarditis.
evidence.source: Dual role of the IL-12/IFN-gamma axis in the development of autoimmune myocarditis: induction by IL-12 and protection by IFN-gamma.

evidence.text: TGF-β-producing CD4(+) T cells play a protective role in F11r(-/-) mice.
evidence.source: Compromised intestinal epithelial barrier induces adaptive immune compensation that protects from colitis.

evidence.text: IgG2a and IgG2b production were increased in the gp130F759/F759 mice.
evidence.source: Dissection of signaling cascades through gp130 in vivo: reciprocal roles for STAT3- and SHP2-mediated signals in immune responses.

evidence.text: Wild-type (WT) mice treated with CX3CR1-blocking reagents show reduced lung disease upon allergen sensitization and challenge.
evidence.source: CX3CR1 is required for airway inflammation by promoting T helper cell survival and maintenance in inflamed lung

evidence.text: IFN-gamma receptor-deficient mice (IFN-gammaR(-/-)) on a BALB/c background immunized with a peptide derived from cardiac alpha-myosin heavy chain develop severe myocarditis.
evidence.source: Lethal autoimmune myocarditis in interferon-gamma receptor-deficient mice: enhanced disease severity by impaired inducible nitric oxide synthase induction.

evidence.text: Interferon-gamma (IFN-gamma) is a cytokine involved in the regulation of inflammatory responses in autoimmune diseases.
evidence.source: Lethal autoimmune myocarditis in interferon-gamma receptor-deficient mice: enhanced disease severity by impaired inducible nitric oxide synthase induction.

evidence.text: Mice were generated with a conditional BCL-2 gene and constitutive c-myc.
evidence.source: Antiapoptotic BCL-2 is required for maintenance of a model leukemia.

evidence.text: The persistent inflammation in IFN-gammaR(-/-) mice was accompanied by vigorous in vitro CD4 T-cell responses.

evidence.text: Blockade of IL-4R signaling did not confer susceptibility to myocarditis in IL-12p40-deficient mice.

evidence.text: Mice deficient in Irp2 were protected from cigarette smoke (CS)-induced experimental COPD.

evidence.text: Absence of IgA in F11r(+/+)Igha(-/-) mice did not affect disease.
